steps.title: Find the shared materials
steps.content: The student's goal is not just to describe Square Tower House. The answer must connect it to modern-day Pueblo buildings. The notes give a direct bridge: Square Tower House was an Ancestral Puebloan settlement with elaborate buildings made mostly of adobe and stone, and buildings in Zuni Pueblo and Isleta Pueblo often still include traditional elements such as adobe and stone.

steps.title: Reject weak connections
steps.content: Choice B uses the shared materials to connect Square Tower House to buildings in modern Pueblo communities. Choice A emphasizes elements modern buildings may include that would not have appeared at Square Tower House, so it stresses difference instead of connection. Choice C falsely says Square Tower House itself includes modern-day elements. Choice D gives location and occupation dates but never links the former settlement to modern Pueblo buildings. The answer is B.
